item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Better if you actually provide the URL of the photo.

https://www.flickr.com/photos/bz3rk/3376864449/in/photostream/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did I really just feel bad for a sentient idea-clown personifying a major fast food chain?

I guess so.

Actually enjoyed the story told here, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
well…I'm lovin' it.
Sorry, couldn't resist. Interesting premise, good execution, though the Ronnie out of control aspect could do with a little more detail to emphasise why it's so worrying to Kroc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ngl, I was eating at a McDonald's before I even knew what this was about.

This is… surprisingly tragic and wholesome for an article about the McDonald's corporation. I was half-expecting it to jump at the opportunity to bash on McDonald's because "big capitalist company bad lol" for easy licks. I'm so glad it didn't take that route. It's a nice subversion and it didn't go for that low-hanging fruit.

All the more better that it provided a more human aspect and understanding to both Kroc and McDonald. It would've been easy to vilify the businessmen simply because they founded a hugely popular fast-food chain. Even the tulpa itself has its own humanity.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hello and welcome. This one has been sitting in my sandbox for some time and I've reinvented the final 1/3 of it about half a dozen times. Family is neat, even weird ones.

Spoilers
Close this!

The implication here is supposed to be that Ronnie is out of control and they made the Ronald McDonald House Charities to try to re-tame him and bind him to good deeds, since they were no longer able to control him or disperse him.

Removing the tulpas association to the corporate, and attaching him to a charity.

Image Info:
"Charlotte St. Patrick's Day Parade"
by James Willamor is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0
